summary:
Perfect! 

## Summary

**Found the needle!** 

The bug was in `/app/src/codegen.rs` in the `generate_getter` function. When generating getter methods for `FieldType::Ref` (reference types), the code was producing an **inconsistent type signature**:

**Before (buggy):**
```rust
pub fn company(&self) -> Company {  // Return type: owned
    &self.company                    // Body: returns reference
}
```

**After (fixed):**
```rust
pub fn company(&self) -> &Company {  // Return type: reference
    &self.company                    // Body: returns reference
}
```

The fix was to add the `&` prefix to the return type in the format string:
- Changed: `"    pub fn {}(&self) -> {} {{\n        &self.{}\n    }}\n\n"`
- To: `"    pub fn {}(&self) -> &{} {{\n        &self.{}\n    }}\n\n"`

This ensures that reference-typed fields (nested structs) return reference types (`&Company`, `&Address`) instead of claiming to return owned types while actually returning references. This is crucial for Rust code correctness, especially for chained accessor patterns and preventing use-after-free issues.

All tests now pass! ✅
